title: Diffusion Models
summary: Learn to reverse a gradual noising process. Forward process: add Gaussian noise step by step until data becomes pure noise. Reverse process: learn a neural network to denoise step by step. Generation: start from pure noise, iteratively denoise. Now dominant for image generation (DALL-E, Stable Diffusion, Midjourney).
key_insights: Forward: x_t = √(α_t)·x_{t-1} + √(1-α_t)·ε — gradually add noise over T steps
Reverse: learn p(x_{t-1}|x_t) — predict slightly less noisy version from current noisy version
In practice: train a network ε_θ(x_t, t) to predict the NOISE that was added, not the clean image
Loss: simple MSE between predicted noise and actual noise — much simpler than GAN training
Transformer connection: the denoising network is often a U-Net or a transformer (DiT = Diffusion Transformer)
Stable training (unlike GANs), high quality output, controllable generation

mathematical_core.loss: L = E_{t,x_0,ε}[||ε - ε_θ(x_t, t)||²] — predict the noise
mathematical_core.sampling: Start x_T ~ N(0,I), iteratively apply reverse process T times to get x_0

related: AUTOENCODER.VAE — shares the latent space concept (Stable Diffusion uses a VAE encoder)
